label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. There are five dancers: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each of them starts with a partner: - Alice with Lola - Bob with Karl - Claire with Patrick - Dave with Sam - Eve with Helga Then there are a series of partner switches. I need to track each switch step by step to figure out who Bob is dancing with at the end. Let's go through each step one by one. First switch: Dave and Alice switch partners. So initially, Dave is with Sam and Alice is with Lola. After switching, Dave will be with Lola and Alice will be with Sam. Let me note that down: After first switch: - Alice: Sam - Dave: Lola Others remain the same: Bob with Karl, Claire with Patrick, Eve with Helga. Second switch: Eve and Alice switch partners. Currently, Eve is with Helga and Alice is with Sam. After switching, Eve will be with Sam and Alice will be with Helga. After second switch: - Alice: Helga - Eve: Sam Others: Bob with Karl, Claire with Patrick, Dave with Lola. Third switch: Bob and Dave switch partners. Bob is with Karl, Dave is with Lola. After switching, Bob will be with Lola and Dave will be with Karl. After third switch: - Bob: Lola - Dave: Karl Others: Alice with Helga, Claire with Patrick, Eve with Sam. Fourth switch: Bob and Eve switch partners. Bob is with Lola, Eve is with Sam. After switching, Bob will be with Sam and Eve will be with Lola. After fourth switch: - Bob: Sam - Eve: Lola Others: Alice with Helga, Claire with Patrick, Dave with Karl. Fifth switch: Bob and Claire switch partners. Bob is with Sam, Claire is with Patrick. After switching, Bob will be with Patrick and Claire will be with Sam. After fifth switch: - Bob: Patrick - Claire: Sam Others: Alice with Helga, Dave with Karl, Eve with Lola. So at the end of the dance, Bob is dancing with Patrick. Looking at the options, Patrick is option (C). Wait, let me double-check each step to make sure I didn't make a mistake. 1. First switch: Dave (Sam) and Alice (Lola) swap. So Alice-Sam, Dave-Lola. Correct. 2. Second switch: Eve (Helga) and
